behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model-output quality from a real speech-to-text model (speaker diarization/label preservation), not just wrapper logic. MOCK-HIDE applies: a stub transcript with perfect labels would hide real diarization failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior specifies one model judgment (label preservation), not multiple agentic handoffs in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a test against the real speech-to-text API using a multi-speaker meeting recording with known speaker turns, then assert speaker labels are preserved in the transcript and remain usable for attendee attribution in downstream summary input.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is local parsing/structuring logic on transcript text; failure is inside one function/object boundary. No required OS/network/subprocess/real model dependency in the behavior text. Rejected Integration: no required module handoff is stated beyond parser output.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the assignment parser with transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments and edge cases; assert exact structured output fields for assignee and task text.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real LLM includes/omits in generated summary content. Deterministic stubs would always pass and miss omission failures. Rejected Workflow: this assertion targets one summary-generation decision quality outcome, not a required multi-agentic chain assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM summarization on transcripts with known per-attendee assignments; assert each attendee assigned a task is represented by at least one action item in live output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text scopes to routing logic once decisions are already identified; this is deterministic mapping to schema fields. Rejected Integration: no required real infra or multi-module failure boundary is explicit in the behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the routing/mapping function with pre-identified decisions and narrative payloads; assert decisions populate only the `decisions` schema fields and not generic narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model output placement/quality (whether key decisions are surfaced in the right section), which depends on real LLM behavior. MOCK-HIDE applies because canned outputs can force correct placement. Rejected Workflow: still a single summary quality judgment, not multiple agentic stages that must all succeed.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM summarization on transcripts containing explicit key decisions; assert those decisions appear in the `decisions` section and are absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: behavior explicitly requires split -> sequential processing -> merge, which is a multi-step pipeline. Boundary signal: correctness depends on chained model calls across segments and final combined summary quality. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: an Integration test with fixed stubs can validate chunk orchestration but misses real cross-segment coherence failures from sequential model outputs. Rejected System: real OS/network alone is insufficient; the dominant risk is multi-agentic step interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end long-audio scenario (>60 min) with real STT+LLM calls per segment and real merge step; assert all segments are processed in order and final summary is coherent (no dropped/contradictory action items or decisions across segment boundaries).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a full multi-stage pipeline with real meeting input and both STT + LLM steps, so MULTI-STEP and LLM-DEP both apply. Evidence: upload -> transcribe -> summarize -> deliver is a sequence of dependent stages. Boundary signal: multiple model-driven transitions can fail only in full chain. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: System-only single-boundary checks cannot catch cross-stage agentic handoff failures end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end run on a real meeting recording: upload audio, perform real transcription, real summarization, and structured delivery; assert completion without manual intervention and valid final schema output.
